questions.question: What is a timeline?
questions.correct_answer: A sequence of dates and events covering any particular period
questions.explanation: Helps in understanding the order in which historical events take place

questions.question: How many years ago was the birth of Buddha?
questions.explanation: Calculated by adding 560 BCE to 2024 CE and subtracting 1

questions.question: What is a century?
questions.explanation: In history, specific centuries are counted every 100 years starting from the year 1 CE

questions.question: Which calendar is commonly used worldwide?
questions.explanation: Has 12 months adding up to 365 days and a leap year every four years

questions.question: What is the difference between BCE and CE?
questions.correct_answer: BCE means Before Common Era, while CE means Common Era
questions.explanation: Used to count dates before and after the conventional date for Jesus' birth

questions.question: What is a millennium?
questions.explanation: In history, specific centuries are counted every 1,000 years from the year 1 CE

questions.question: How many years did it take for the ice to melt after the last Ice Age?
questions.explanation: After the last Ice Age, living conditions improved for humans and they started settling down and cultivating cereals and grains

questions.question: What is rock art?
questions.explanation: First examples of rock art in the world were during the last Ice Age

questions.question: How many centuries are there between 2000 BCE and 100 CE?
questions.explanation: Counting every 100 years, starting from 1 BCE

questions.question: What is a pañchānga?
questions.correct_answer: A book of tables listing the days of each month along with related astronomical data
questions.explanation: Traditionally used in India to predict events like solar and lunar eclipses, times for sunrise and sunset, etc.

important_notes.title: Timeline
important_notes.content: A convenient tool to mark historical events in sequence

important_notes.title: Century
important_notes.content: Any period of 100 years; specific centuries are counted every 100 years starting from the year 1 CE

important_notes.title: Millennium
important_notes.content: Any period of 1,000 years; specific centuries are counted every 1,000 years from the year 1 CE

important_notes.title: Gregorian Calendar
important_notes.content: Commonly used worldwide; has 12 months adding up to 365 days and a leap year every four years

important_notes.title: BCE and CE
important_notes.content: BCE means Before Common Era, while CE means Common Era; used to count dates before and after the conventional date for Jesus' birth

important_notes.title: Pañchānga
important_notes.content: Traditionally used in India to predict events like solar and lunar eclipses, times for sunrise and sunset, etc.

important_notes.title: Rock Art
important_notes.content: Examples of auspicious beginnings; first examples were during the last Ice Age

important_notes.title: Timeline Usage
important_notes.content: Helps in understanding the order in which historical events take place

important_notes.title: Century Counting
important_notes.content: Counting every 100 years, starting from 1 BCE

important_notes.title: Millennium Counting
important_notes.content: Counting every 1,000 years, starting from 1 CE

facts.fact: The last Ice Age lasted for over 100,000 years and ended around 12,000 years ago.
Humans started settling down and cultivating cereals and grains after the last Ice Age.
The Gregorian calendar is commonly used worldwide and has 12 months adding up to 365 days and a leap year every four years.
The BCE and CE systems are used to count dates before and after the conventional date for Jesus' birth.
Pañchāngas, traditionally used in India, predict events like solar and lunar eclipses, times for sunrise and sunset, etc.
Rock art is an example of auspicious beginnings and first appeared during the last Ice Age.
The 21st century CE runs from 2001 to 2100.
The 3rd millennium CE began in 2001 CE and will go up to 3000 CE.
A pañchānga is a book of tables listing the days of each month along with related astronomical data.
The year 2000 CE was a leap year because it is a multiple of 400.
